attempts.proof_body:
intro x xs
  induction xs with
  | nil =>
      intro _
      simp [insort, sorted]
  | cons a xs ih =>
      cases xs with
      | nil =>
          intro _
          simp [insort, sorted]
      | cons b ts =>
          intro hsorted
          -- unpack sortedness of a::b::ts
          have hab : a ≤ b := by
            simpa [sorted] using (And.left (by simpa [sorted] using hsorted))
          have htail : sorted (b :: ts) := by
            simpa [sorted] using (And.right (by simpa [sorted] using hsorted))
          -- now split on x ≤ a
          by_cases hxa : x ≤ a
          · -- insert before a
            simp [insort, hxa, sorted, hxa, hab, htail]
          · -- insert after a
            have hrec : sorted (insort x (b :: ts)) := ih (b :: ts) htail
            -- show a ≤ head of insort x (b::ts)
            have ha_head : a ≤ (insort x (b :: ts)).headD 0 := by
              -- analyze insort at the next step
              by_cases hxb : x ≤ b
              · -- then head is x
                have ax : a ≤ x := by
                  have hxlt : a < x := Nat.lt_of_not_ge hxa
                  exact Nat.le_of_lt hxlt
                simp [insort, hxb, ax]
              · -- then head is b
                simp [insort, hxb, hab]
            -- finish: sorted (a :: insort x (b::ts))
            -- use the characterization of sorted for lists with ≥2 elements
            cases hins : insort x (b :: ts) with
            | nil =>
                -- impossible: insort never returns []
                simp [insort] at hins
            | cons c cs =>
                -- show sorted (a::c::cs)
                have hac : a ≤ c := by
                  -- rewrite headD fact using the computed head
                  simpa [hins] using ha_head
                have hsorted_tail : sorted (c :: cs) := by
                  simpa [hins] using hrec
                simp [insort, hxa, sorted, hins, hac, hsorted_tail]

-- This statement is not provable from the given definitions alone:
-- `sorted` is a `Bool`, so the goal is a proposition `sorted (sort xs)`,
-- which would require `sorted (sort xs) = True` or coercions not provided here.
-- Moreover, proving insertion-sort correctness needs lemmas about `insort`
-- preserving sortedness, which are not available in the local context.
--
-- If `sorted` were defined as a Prop (or you had lemmas connecting the Bool to Prop),
-- the proof would proceed by induction on `xs`.
--
-- As-is, Lean cannot close this goal without additional assumptions/lemmas.
cases xs <;> simp [sort, insort, sorted]

attempts.proof_body:
-- prove a local lemma: inserting into a sorted list yields a sorted list
  have insort_sorted : ∀ (n : Nat) (ys : List Nat), sorted ys = true → sorted (insort n ys) = true := by
    intro n ys
    induction ys with
    | nil =>
        intro _
        simp [insort, sorted]
    | cons y ys ih =>
        cases ys with
        | nil =>
            intro _
            simp [insort, sorted]
        | cons z zs =>
            intro hsorted
            -- unfold sorted on (y::z::zs)
            simp [sorted] at hsorted
            -- hsorted : (y <= z && sorted (z :: zs)) = true
            have hyz : y ≤ z := by
              -- extract left conjunct from boolean and
              have : y ≤ z ∧ sorted (z :: zs) = true := by
                simpa [Bool.and_eq_true] using hsorted
              exact this.1
            have htail : sorted (z :: zs) = true := by
              have : y ≤ z ∧ sorted (z :: zs) = true := by
                simpa [Bool.and_eq_true] using hsorted
              exact this.2
            -- now analyze the insertion position
            by_cases hn : n ≤ y
            · -- insert before y
              simp [insort, hn, sorted, hn]
            · -- insert after y
              have hn' : ¬ n ≤ y := hn
              -- insort n (y::z::zs) = y :: insort n (z::zs)
              -- need to show y <= head of insort n (z::zs) and tail sorted
              -- first, show sorted (insort n (z::zs)) = true by IH on (z::zs)
              have hrec : sorted (insort n (z :: zs)) = true := ih (z :: zs) htail
              -- show y <= head of insort n (z::zs)
              -- head is either n (if n<=z) or z otherwise; both >= y using hyz
              by_cases hnz : n ≤ z
              · -- head becomes n, need y ≤ n; but since ¬n≤y and Nat.le_total, we get y ≤ n
                have hyn : y ≤ n := by
                  have := Nat.le_total y n
                  cases this with
                  | inl h => exact h
                  | inr h =>
                      exfalso
                      exact hn' (le_of_eq (Nat.le_antisymm h (Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.lt_of_le_of_ne h (by
                        intro hEq; cases hEq)))))
                -- simpler: from total order, ¬ n ≤ y implies y ≤ n
                -- replace with standard lemma:
                -- (we keep the above but it's messy); use `Nat.le_of_not_ge`? doesn't exist.
                -- We'll redo cleanly:
                clear hyn
                have hyn : y ≤ n := by
                  have := Nat.le_total y n
                  cases this with
                  | inl h => exact h
                  | inr h =>
                      -- h : n ≤ y contradicts hn'
                      exact False.elim (hn' h)
                simp [insort, hn', sorted, hnz, hyz, hyn, hrec]
              · -- head stays z, need y ≤ z which is hyz
                simp [insort, hn', sorted, hnz, hyz, hrec]
  -- main proof by induction on xs
  induction xs with
  | nil =>
      simp [sort, sorted]
  | cons x xs ih =>
      -- ih : sorted (sort xs) = true
      exact insort_sorted x (sort xs) ih

have insort_sorted :
      ∀ (n : Nat) (ys : List Nat), sorted ys = true → sorted (insort n ys) = true := by
    intro n ys
    induction ys with
    | nil =>
        intro _
        simp [insort, sorted]
    | cons y ys ih =>
        cases ys with
        | nil =>
            intro _
            by_cases hny : n ≤ y
            · simp [insort, sorted, hny]
            · simp [insort, sorted, hny]
        | cons z zs =>
            intro hsorted
            -- turn the boolean condition into propositional facts
            have hyz : y ≤ z := by
              have : (y ≤ z) ∧ (sorted (z :: zs) = true) := by
                simpa [sorted, Bool.and_eq_true] using hsorted
              exact this.1
            have htail : sorted (z :: zs) = true := by
              have : (y ≤ z) ∧ (sorted (z :: zs) = true) := by
                simpa [sorted, Bool.and_eq_true] using hsorted
              exact this.2
            by_cases hny : n ≤ y
            · -- insert before y
              simp [insort, sorted, hny, hyz, htail]
            · -- insert after y: result is y :: insort n (z::zs)
              have hrec : sorted (insort n (z :: zs)) = true := ih htail
              by_cases hnz : n ≤ z
              · -- head of tail becomes n, need y ≤ n
                have hyn : y ≤ n := by
                  have ht := Nat.le_total y n
                  cases ht with
                  | inl h => exact h
                  | inr h => exact False.elim (hny h)
                simp [insort, sorted, hny, hnz, hyn, hrec]
              · -- head of tail stays z, need y ≤ z
                simp [insort, sorted, hny, hnz, hyz, hrec]
  induction xs with
  | nil =>
      simp [sort, sorted]
  | cons x xs ih =>
      exact insort_sorted x (sort xs) ih

induction xs generalizing ys with
  | nil =>
      intro h
      cases ys with
      | nil =>
          simp [length, reverse, zip']
      | cons y ys =>
          cases (Nat.noConfusion (by simpa [length] using h) : False)
  | cons x xs ih =>
      intro h
      cases ys with
      | nil =>
          cases (Nat.noConfusion (by simpa [length] using h) : False)
      | cons y ys =>
          have h' : length xs = length ys := by
            simpa [length] using h
          have ih' := ih ys h'
          -- auxiliary lemma: zip' respects appending singletons when lengths match
          have hz :
              zip' (reverse xs ++ [x]) (reverse ys ++ [y]) =
                zip' (reverse xs) (reverse ys) ++ [(x, y)] := by
            -- prove a more general statement by induction on xs
            revert ys
            induction xs with
            | nil =>
                intro ys hlen
                cases ys with
                | nil =>
                    simp [reverse, zip']
                | cons y' ys' =>
                    cases (Nat.noConfusion (by simpa [length] using hlen) : False)
            | cons x' xs ih2 =>
                intro ys hlen
                cases ys with
                | nil =>
                    cases (Nat.noConfusion (by simpa [length] using hlen) : False)
                | cons y' ys' =>
                    have hlen' : length xs = length ys' := by
                      simpa [length] using hlen
                    have := ih2 ys' hlen'
                    -- unfold reverse/zip' once and use associativity
                    simp [reverse, zip', this, List.append_assoc]
          -- finish using IH and the auxiliary lemma
          simpa [reverse, zip', ih', hz, List.append_assoc]

ih : ∀ (m : ℕ) (xs : List α), drop n (take m xs) = take (m - n) (drop n xs)
n✝ : ℕ
⊢ take (n✝ - n) [] = []
